message:
Add scenario composer and run-queue manager contract to doctor subsystem

Introduce a deterministic scenario-composer contract (doctor-scenario-
composer-v1) that defines how doctor scenarios are templated, composed
into queue entries, dispatched with priority ordering, and validated.

New types:
- ScenarioComposerContract: top-level contract with template catalog,
  queue policy, and failure taxonomy
- ScenarioTemplate: defines command classes, artifact requirements,
  priority, retry budget, and replay-seed requirements per scenario
- ScenarioRunQueuePolicy: max concurrency, queue depth, dispatch order,
  priority bands, and cancellation policy
- ScenarioRunRequest: inbound request with run_id, template_id,
  correlation_id, seed, and optional priority override
- ScenarioRunQueueEntry: composed queue entry ready for dispatch
- ScenarioQueueFailureClass: structured failure codes with severity,
  retryability, and required operator action

New functions:
- scenario_composer_contract(): returns canonical contract with three
  built-in templates (happy_path_smoke, regression_bundle,
  cancel_recovery) and four failure classes
- validate_scenario_composer_contract(): validates template ordering,
  command class cross-references against the execution adapter, queue
  policy constraints, and failure taxonomy completeness
- compose_scenario_run(): composes a single queue entry from a request,
  validating template existence, seed requirements, and slug formats
- build_scenario_run_queue(): batch-composes requests into a priority-
  sorted queue with depth-limit enforcement
- dispatch_scenario_run_queue(): applies concurrency limits by marking
  top-N entries as "running" and remainder as "queued"

Also includes:
- Outputtable impl for ScenarioComposerContract with human-readable
  formatting of templates and queue policy
- #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] on execution_adapter_contract()
- Idiomatic map_or_else in plan_execution_command route fallback
- Modernized format string in advance_execution_state error path
- Seven new tests: contract validation, determinism, JSON round-trip,
  unknown command class rejection, default priority composition,
  priority-then-run_id queue ordering, queue overflow rejection,
  and max-concurrency dispatch enforcement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
